# The expected output for @main(0,0) is "λa.λb.b" 
# Let's trace: @main(λf.λx.x, λf.λx.x) with @main=λa.λb.λc.λd.a(c,b(c,d))
# = (λa.λb.λc.λd.a(c,b(c,d))) (λf.λx.x) (λf.λx.x)
# = λc.λd.(λf.λx.x)(c,(λf.λx.x)(c,d))   <- but (λf.λx.x)(c, ...) = λx.x applied with 2 args
# = λc.λd.(λf.λx.x)(c,(λx.x)(d))
# Hmm wait - (λf.λx.x)(c) = λx.x, then applied to (λx.x)(d) - wait multi-arg means ((f c) d)
# (λf.λx.x)(c) = λx.x, then (λx.x)(b(c,d)) = λx.x... no wait
# 0 = λf.λx.x means: apply f zero times to x. So 0(c,d) = (0 c) d = (λx.x) d = d
# So @main(0,0) = λc.λd.0(c, 0(c,d)) = λc.λd.d — correct!
# But we're getting something different. The issue is we need the FULL file run as the book.

cat > /tmp/test_full.lam << 'EOF'
@main=λa.λb.λc.λd.a(c,b(c,d))
@zero=λf.λx.x
@_=@main(@zero,@zero)
EOF
bun reference/lambench/src/lamb.ts /tmp/test_full.lam 2>&1
